content: today was busy at the office and the launch on Friday is still on my mind. I keep replaying the demo in my head, and I'm not sure I'll be ready in time.
I felt small in the team meeting again. My ideas were good, but I held them back. I wish I could just speak up without rehearsing every sentence first.
the kids were impossible at bedtime. I snapped at the oldest and immediately regretted it. I want to be the kind of parent who breathes before reacting.
spent the morning in the garden, pulling weeds and listening to birds. The basil is starting to come back. It was the calmest hour I have had in weeks.
money is tight again this month. The car repair wiped out the buffer. I am tired of feeling like one bill away from a quiet panic.
tried to pray this morning and could not focus. My mind kept drifting to email. Maybe sitting still IS the prayer; I do not know.
ran four miles before sunrise and felt like myself for the first time in days. The body remembers what the mind forgets.
an old friend texted out of the blue. We are catching up next week. I had not realized how much I missed her until I saw her name on the screen.
my mother called and I let it go to voicemail. I will call her back tomorrow. Or the day after. I keep meaning to be a better daughter.
the new manager keeps rescheduling our one-on-one. I know it is not personal, but it makes me feel invisible. I should ask for what I need directly.
started reading a novel before bed instead of scrolling. Slept better. The phone is louder than I gave it credit for.
watched my son lose his soccer game and try not to cry. He is learning what loss feels like, and I am learning that I cannot protect him from it.
the launch slipped a week. Part of me is relieved, part of me is furious. I think I needed the deadline more than the deadline needed me.
rain all afternoon. I stayed in with a book and a kettle of tea. There was no productivity in any of it, and that felt like a small rebellion.
I am tired of pretending the budget will fix itself. I sat down with the numbers tonight. Painful, but at least the picture is honest now.
had dinner with my partner and did not check my phone once. We laughed about nothing for an hour. I want more nights like this.
the team shipped a hard fix today. I am proud of them. I forget sometimes that the work is also a kind of love.
anxious about the presentation Wednesday. I keep imagining everything going wrong. The catastrophizing voice is loud this week.
started a new sketchbook. Filled four pages of bad drawings. The point is the practice, not the result, and the practice felt good.
the dog has been clingy since the move. He follows me from room to room. I wonder if he can sense how unsettled I still feel.
I keep checking my phone first thing in the morning. I want to break that habit. Tomorrow I am going to leave it on the dresser overnight.
saw my therapist today. She asked me what I would say to a friend in my situation, and the answer was kinder than what I have been saying to myself.
the kids made waffles for breakfast and the kitchen looked like a crime scene. Worth it. They are getting old enough to be useful.
lost my temper in traffic again. I scared myself a little. There is something underneath the anger that I have not figured out yet.
we walked the trail behind the church and watched the sun fall behind the ridge. My partner held my hand the whole way back.
the financial advisor pushed me to lock in a number I am not ready for. I asked for another week. Saying no felt strangely good.
I am behind on the side project again. The day evaporates. Maybe the issue is not time management, maybe it is fear of finishing.
tried meditation for ten minutes. My back hurt and my brain refused to settle. I am still glad I sat down.
the boss complimented my work in front of the room and I did not know where to put my eyes. Why is being seen so hard.
argued with my brother about politics again. Walked it back over text afterwards. We are both tired of being right.
the baby slept through the night for the first time in three weeks. I lay in bed listening for a cry that never came.
ordered groceries instead of going out. Saved twenty dollars and an hour. Tiny wins, stacked, are how I climb out of this.
the air was cold this morning and the coffee tasted like a small mercy. I think I am learning to notice the good before the day eats it.
feeling lonely in a way that surprised me, even with people around all day. There is a particular kind of quiet that crowds make.
wrote two pages of the novel and they were honest. That is more than I have written in a month. I will take it.
the new hire asked a question in the all-hands and I saw myself ten years ago. I made a note to mentor her properly, not just in passing.
we lit a candle for grandma at dinner. The kids asked questions. I told them what I remembered, and the remembering felt like a kind of prayer.
scrolled too long before bed. Woke up tired and resentful of myself. Tomorrow the phone goes in the kitchen drawer at nine.
fixed the leaky faucet by myself with a YouTube video and a wrench. Smaller than it sounds, bigger than it sounds. Both things at once.
I am not sure faith is something I have anymore so much as something I keep returning to. The doubt is part of the practice.
long meeting that should have been a Slack message. I felt my whole afternoon drain out of me. Need to push back next time.
took the kids to the lake. The youngest asked why the water was older than people. I did not have a good answer. I love that.
the doctor said the bloodwork was fine. I cried a little in the car after. Relief is heavier than I expect every time.
drafted the resignation letter and did not send it. It is sitting in drafts. I am not sure if writing it is a step toward leaving or a way of staying.
the partner did the dishes without being asked and I almost cried. We are tired in the same direction these days, which is its own kind of love.
I want to stop saying yes to things I do not want to do. I am going to practice the no out loud before the next ask comes.
the dawn light through the kitchen window felt like a small grace. I am trying to start mornings like this on purpose.
spent the afternoon teaching my daughter to ride without training wheels. She fell. She got back on. I want to remember the look on her face.
anxious about the bank statement. The story I tell about money is louder than the actual numbers. I should look at the numbers.
watched a sermon online that hit harder than I expected. The line about being known and loved at the same time stayed with me all day.
the team retro went sideways. I wish I had spoken up sooner. Silence is a choice and I keep making it.
did the laundry, the dishes, the dog. Nothing remarkable. Some days the holiness IS in the small obediences.
called dad on the way home. He sounded older than last time. I do not know how to ask the questions I need to ask.
felt jealous reading a friend's announcement on social media. Then guilty. Then tired. Closed the app and went outside.
started lifting again after the injury. The numbers are humbling. The body is patient if I am.
the client wants the deck moved up a week. I said yes because I could not figure out how to say anything else. Tomorrow I will renegotiate.
we built a fort in the living room and ate dinner inside it. The kids will remember this. I want to be the kind of adult my kids remember being silly.
I am scared the marriage is harder than it should be at this stage. I do not want to write that. I am going to bring it to therapy next week.
got a real read of the budget tonight. Two months of runway is not the disaster I feared, but it is not nothing. I will draw a plan tomorrow.
an article on attention rewired the way I think about my mornings. The first hour is the day's posture. I want my posture to be still.
picked up the guitar for the first time in months. My fingers remember more than my head does. That is true of a lot of things.
the rain on the windows tonight feels like an old friend. I do not need to do anything about it. It is just here.
I keep snapping at my partner about small things. I think I am angry about something else. I want to find the actual thing and address it.
long run, slow miles, no music. The mind quieted around mile three. The legs were done by mile six. A fair trade.
we hosted neighbors for dinner. The house felt full in a way it has not in a while. I want to do this once a month, easy.
anxious about the launch demo on Friday. I keep walking through the failure modes. Need to remember the team has my back.
I told my therapist about the anger and she did not flinch. She asked what it was protecting. I am still sitting with the question.
watched the sunrise from the porch with coffee and no phone. Twelve minutes. The day felt like mine before the world arrived.
the kids asked if we could pray for grandma at bedtime. I forget how natural it is for them. I am still learning from people half my size.
spent two hours debugging a config error that turned out to be a missing comma. Humbling. Software is mostly noticing the small thing.
cleaned the garage. Donated the boxes that have moved with us three times unopened. Felt lighter for an hour after.
the manager said I was on track for promotion. I should be excited. I am tired. I will sit with both.
took the youngest to the bookstore and let her pick anything. She picked four. I bought all four. Some days you just buy the books.
the mortgage application is finally in. I do not want to think about money again until they call. I will think about money again tomorrow.
ran into an old colleague at the coffee shop. We talked for forty minutes. Friendship is mostly proximity and showing up.
I have been hiding from the journal because I do not want to write the hard sentence. The hard sentence is that I am lonely in my own house.
we tried a new church on Sunday. The music was good, the sermon was earnest, and nobody asked me to sign up for anything. I will go back.
wrote a hard email to a vendor. Sent it. The hours of dread were worse than the email. They almost always are.
the dog ate part of the rug. I laughed first, then sighed, then ordered a new rug. Children of all species are expensive.
I am tired of the side hustle. I do not know if that means quit or means rest. The two answers feel different and I cannot tell which is which.
the partner and I picked names for a hypothetical second kid. Neither of us is sure we want one. Naming it made the question feel real.
got laid off in the morning. Wrote this in the afternoon. I am scared, and a little relieved, and trying to be honest about both.
the new role posting hit my inbox and I do not know if I want it. I keep waiting to know. Maybe knowing is a thing you do, not a thing you wait for.
took the kids to the science museum. The youngest stood in front of the planet exhibit for twenty minutes. I want to live like that.
ran the numbers on the move and it works. Not easily, but it works. We are going to do the thing.
an old photo turned up in a drawer and I sat on the floor with it for ten minutes. Time goes faster when you are inside it.
the kids fought all morning. By noon I had given up on the schedule. By two I had given up on the morality lesson. By four I was just trying to feed everyone.
started journaling longhand again. The hand is slower, which is the point. The thoughts catch up.
called my best friend about the layoff. She did not try to fix it. She just listened. That was the right answer.
watched the sunset from the back step. Nothing happened. That was the gift.
the resume is updated. The LinkedIn is updated. The hardest part is writing as if I believe in myself, but I am practicing.
the partner suggested couples counseling. I said yes faster than I expected. We are trying. That counts.
long walk with the dog after dinner. The neighborhood was quiet. I remembered how much I like this place when I slow down enough to see it.
I am praying for patience and being given more chances to practice it. The teacher has a sense of humor.
did the laundry while listening to a podcast about ancient stoics. Marcus Aurelius would have loved a tumble dryer. I am sure of it.
took a sick day for the first time in months. Slept eleven hours. The body keeps a longer ledger than I want to admit.
the launch went well. The demo did not crash. I am proud of the team and a little tearful. Pride and grief are sometimes the same wave.
started a savings envelope for a real vacation, not a wishful one. Twenty dollars in. It is not the amount, it is the act.
wrote a thank you note to a teacher I had in eighth grade. I do not know if I will send it. Writing it was already most of the point.
the rain stopped at dusk and the light turned gold. I called the kids to the porch. We did not say much. We will remember this.
